solution.answer: $H_0 \cong \mathbb{Z},\quad H_1 \cong \mathbb{Z}/2,\quad H_2 = 0,\quad H_3 \cong \mathbb{Z}/2,\quad H_4 = 0,\quad H_n = 0 \text{ for } n \geq 5.$
steps: Chain complex for $\mathbb{R}P^4$: $0 \to C_4 \xrightarrow{\times 2} C_3 \xrightarrow{0} C_2 \xrightarrow{\times 2} C_1 \xrightarrow{0} C_0 \to 0$, each $C_k = \mathbb{Z}$.
$H_0 = \mathbb{Z}$. Note: since $4$ is even, $\mathbb{R}P^4$ is non-orientable ($H_4 = 0$), unlike $\mathbb{R}P^3$.
